posts.text: 𝐔𝐊 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐭 𝐒𝐧𝐚𝐩𝐬𝐡𝐨𝐭 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 QUBA Solutions Ltd The latest market data suggests a familiar story for many recruiters: 📈 The market isn't getting worse. 📉 But it isn't fully recovering either. According to QUBA's latest Recruitment Market Snapshot: 🔸 Permanent placements are still declining, but at the slowest rate in 3 years 🔸 Temp billings remain under pressure, although the decline is easing 🔸 Candidate availability continues to rise 🔸 Vacancy declines are slowing 🔸 Client confidence remains cautious The result? A market that's stabilising, but where hiring decisions are taking longer and cash flow management remains critical. As QUBA's Rik King puts it: "Hiring hasn't stopped, it's become more cautious, more considered, and more competitive." For agencies, success in this environment means moving quickly, adding value beyond CVs, and maintaining strong operational and financial foundations. 📖 Read the full market snapshot 👇 https://lnkd.in/ep3mAV-H #RecruitmentMarket #RecruitmentIndustry #AgencyGrowth #RecruitmentLeadership #CashFlow #UKRecruiter

posts.text: 𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐋𝐀𝐖 𝐂𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐛𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐬 𝟓 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐒𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐬 Disputes and unpaid fees remain one of the biggest frustrations in recruitment. Over the last five years, recLAW has supported agencies across the UK to resolve disputes and recover unpaid fees, handling 3,400+ disputes and recovering more than £13.3 million. Some key reminders from the article: 🔸 Many disputes are recoverable with the right approach 🔸 Early action can significantly improve outcomes 🔸 Clear terms of business matter more than many realise 🔸Small contractual details can make a big difference in high-value disputes The takeaway? Protecting your fees often comes down to timing, precision, and acting early. Congratulations to recLAW on five years of supporting the recruitment industry 👏 📖 Read the full article 👇 https://lnkd.in/e7bRS3Ja #RecruitmentLaw #UnpaidFees #RecruitmentIndustry #LegalSupport #RecruitmentLeadership #UKRecruiter

posts.text: Last year, 210 job board leaders from 27 countries joined us in London for the Job Boards Connect Annual Conference. On 8 October, we'll come together again to tackle the challenges and opportunities shaping the future of our industry. The job board market is changing faster than ever: • AI is reshaping how candidates search and apply for jobs • Traffic acquisition is becoming more competitive and expensive • Traditional business models are under increasing pressure That's why we're bringing together job board founders, owners and senior leaders to share what's working, what isn't, and where the biggest opportunities lie for growth 8 October 2026 in London Early Bird tickets are now available: https://lnkd.in/eJ4UfSe9 If you're planning to join us, now is a good time to get the date in your diary.
posts.hook: Last year, 210 job board leaders from 27 countries joined us in London for the Job Boards Connect Annual Conference.
posts.name: Louise Triance

posts.text: A quick shout-out for someone I have huge respect for. My friend Cinzia Ricciardone is currently open to new fractional and advisory opportunities. I've known Cinz for years through the recruitment and HR technology space, and she's one of those rare people who combines strategic thinking with the ability to actually get things done. With experience across product leadership, operations, SaaS, recruitment technology and scaling businesses, she helps organisations tackle challenges around product strategy, operational effectiveness, tech stack decisions, team performance and growth. She is also an incredibly kind, generous and fun human being! She's worked with businesses including Broadbean, Cognism, SmartRecruiters and Hire Genius, bringing over 15 years of product management experience and 12 years of product leadership experience. If you're looking for a Fractional Chief Product & Operations Officer, or know someone who is, I'd be very happy to make an introduction. Drop me a message or connect with Cinzia directly.
posts.hook: A quick shout-out for someone I have huge respect for.
posts.name: Louise Triance

posts.text: 𝐆𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐁𝐥𝐨𝐠: 𝐇𝐨𝐰 𝐭𝐨 𝐌𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐂𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐫 𝐒𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐬𝐟𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 - 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 3R In recruitment, customer satisfaction isn’t just a “nice to have”, it directly impacts retention, repeat business and referrals. For agencies looking to scale, maintaining strong client relationships is critical. In this latest guest blog, 3R explores: 🔸 How recruitment agencies measure customer satisfaction 🔸 Why client happiness drives growth 🔸The key KPIs and metrics to track 🔸When to collect feedback, and what to do with it 🔸 Digital tools that can support the process Agencies that measure and act on client satisfaction are far better positioned for sustainable growth. 📖 Read the full article 👇 https://lnkd.in/eMGzJZw8 #CustomerSatisfaction #ClientExperience #RecruitmentGrowth #AgencyGrowth #RecruitmentLeadership #UKRecruiter
